title: Lastrea divergens

This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea divergens include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lastrea divergens include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Lastrea divergens is classified within the family Blechnaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the beech ferns. This species belongs to the genus Lastrea, which is a part of the larger division Pteridophyta. The family Blechnaceae is further grouped under the class Polypodiopsida and the division Pteridophyta. The class Polypodiopsida is then a part of the larger kingdom Plantae. Lastrea divergens is also sometimes classified as Blechnum divergens within the same family.
